attack_surface.data_description: Claim request including proof and amount

security_gap.gap_description: Claim proof validation vulnerability allows attacker to claim excessive or unearned incentives.

actor_risk.description: External user attempting to exploit claim mechanism for illicit gain.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Flaws in off-chain proof generation or on-chain validation could lead to theft of incentives.

assumptions: The process for generating or validating claim proofs contains a vulnerability.
An attacker can manipulate their claim proof or generate one for unearned incentives.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust cryptographic proof verification on-chain for incentive claims.

description: An attacker manipulates the claim proof or input data when claiming incentives from the BGTIncentiveDistributor contract, allowing them to steal tokens.

attack_surface.data_description: Vote parameters including proposal ID, vote choice, and vote weight

security_gap.gap_description: Weak validation of caller's voting power allows vote tampering or unauthorized voting.

actor_risk.description: External user attempting to manipulate governance outcomes by casting fraudulent votes.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Failure to validate vote weight against authorized BGT balance or delegation can subvert governance.

assumptions: The governance contract does not correctly validate the voting power associated with a vote.
An attacker can craft a transaction to cast a vote with more power than their BGT balance or delegation allows.

mitigation.strategy: Enforce strict on-chain validation of BGT balance and delegation for determining voting power.

description: An attacker sends manipulated vote transactions with inflated voting power to unfairly influence the outcome of governance proposals.

attack_surface.data_description: Swap transaction parameters (token pair, amounts, pool)

security_gap.gap_description: BEX swap function is vulnerable to transaction spam, disrupting trading or increasing gas costs.

actor_risk.description: External user attempting to disrupt BEX service through transaction flooding.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume of transactions could overwhelm the network or BEX contract processing, impacting availability.

assumptions: An attacker has sufficient resources to send a large volume of swap transactions.
The network or BEX contract is not fully resilient against transaction spam.

mitigation.strategy: Implement transaction rate limiting and monitor for suspicious activity.

description: An attacker floods the BEX swap function with excessive transactions, causing delays, increased fees, or temporary service disruption for legitimate users.

attack_surface.data_description: Mint request parameters (collateral type/amount, requested HONEY)

security_gap.gap_description: HoneySwap minting function is vulnerable to transaction spam, disrupting stablecoin supply or increasing fees.

actor_risk.description: External user attempting to disrupt HONEY minting through transaction flooding.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A high volume of mint transactions could strain the HoneySwap contract or network, impacting availability.

assumptions: An attacker has sufficient resources to send a large volume of mint transactions.
The network or HoneySwap contract lacks sufficient resilience to transaction flooding.

mitigation.strategy: Implement transaction rate limiting and monitor for suspicious minting activity.

description: An attacker floods the HoneySwap minting function with transactions, causing delays, increased fees, or disruption to the process of minting the native stablecoin.

attack_surface.data_description: Boost request including Validator ID and BGT amount

security_gap.gap_description: Vulnerability in boost mechanism allows a user to apply boost using BGT they do not legitimately control.

actor_risk.description: External user attempting to gain unauthorized influence over validator boost using illicit BGT.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Incorrect validation of BGT ownership or delegation allows unauthorized influence on validator emissions.

assumptions: The validator boosting contract incorrectly validates ownership or delegation rights for the BGT being used.
An attacker can craft a transaction to apply boost with BGT they are not entitled to use.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strict on-chain validation of BGT ownership or delegation rights before applying boost.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the validator boosting smart contract to gain unearned influence over a validator's BGT emissions by using BGT they don't control.
